description: In a dimly lit restaurant, Chandler and Susie sit closely together at a table. Chandler says, "Um," looking at Susie.
The two maintain their close proximity at the table.
Susie leans closer to Chandler, her gaze fixed on him. "How come all I can think about is putting that ice in my mouth and licking you all over?" she asks softly.
Chandler stares at Susie with his lips parted. Audience laughter is audible.
"Because I went to an all-boys high school and God is making up for it," Chandler says. Audience laughter continues.
Susie maintains her gaze on Chandler, a slight smile on her face. Chandler keeps his eyes on her as the audience laughs.
The two remain seated closely, looking at each other in silence.
"I want you right here, right now," Susie says, leaning her head closer to his.
Chandler maintains eye contact with Susie.
Chandler replies, "Right now, right here."
Chandler slightly shifts his posture.
Chandler points his index finger toward the surrounding room and says, "Don't you think we're in kind of a public play?"
At the same table, Ross, Joey, and a woman in a shiny shirt sit together, holding open menus.
Chandler, now holding a menu, says, "They do have the shrimp."
Ross, Joey, and the woman in the shiny shirt look at their menus. Chandler and Susie then sit closely together at the table.
"Meet me in the bathroom," Susie whispers to Chandler, placing her hand on his shoulder as she stands.
Susie walks away from the table. Chandler turns his head to watch her leave.
Chandler sits with Ross, Joey, and the woman in the shiny shirt. He tosses his menu onto the table.
Chandler abruptly stands up, leaning forward. "I'm going to the bathroom now," he announces loudly.
Chandler quickly walks away. Ross, Joey, and the woman in the shiny shirt turn their heads to watch him depart.
The woman in the shiny shirt and Joey turn their gaze toward Ross, who looks back at them.
"He likes to keep us in the loop," Ross says, looking toward the woman in the shiny shirt.
The setting changes to a public restroom. Chandler and Susie stand in the middle of the room, kissing with their arms wrapped around each other.
They stop kissing. Susie steps backward toward an open bathroom stall while holding onto Chandler.
Susie pulls Chandler into the stall with her and pulls the door closed.
From inside the closed stall, Chandler's voice says, "Can't believe we're doing this." Susie's voice follows, saying, "All right, Mr." Their feet are visible in the gap below the door.
The two remain inside the closed stall.
"Let's see those panties," Susie's voice says from inside the stall.
Chandler's dark trousers fall to the floor around his ankles.
"All right," Chandler says.
Susie's bare foot reaches over and briefly touches Chandler's leg. Audience laughter is audible.
"But you know what would be even sexier?" Susie says.
Susie's foot withdraws.
"What?" Chandler asks.
"If you didn't have your shirt tucked into them," Susie says.
Chandler's feet shift slightly on the floor.
"All right. Now I would like to see you wearing nothing but them," Susie says.
"Take your clothes off," Susie commands.
"Okay, but I hope you realize this means we're going to miss hearing about the specials," Chandler says. His dark shirt and other clothing fall to the floor around his ankles.
Susie pushes the stall door open and steps out, barefoot, holding her shoes and a black garment.
"Come on, hurry, hurry," Susie says, standing outside the stall. "Hey, do you want this done quick or do you want it done right?" Chandler responds from inside.
"All right, turn around," Susie says.
"I will see you from behind," Susie continues, pointing toward the stall.
Chandler's bare feet shift as he turns around inside the stall. Susie stands facing the door.
Susie leans her hands against the stall door, looking over the top. "Somebody's been doing his Buns of Steel videos," she says.
"So you want me to clench anything or" Chandler says. Susie steps away from the door.
Susie bends down and reaches under the stall door, pulling Chandler's discarded clothes out from around his ankles.
"Susie," Chandler says from inside.
Susie stands up rapidly, holding his clothes bunched in her arms.
"Susie," Chandler repeats.
Susie gathers the garments tightly to her chest, her mouth open.
"This is for the fourth," Susie shouts toward the stall, pointing her finger forcefully.
Susie turns and runs out of the bathroom. Chandler drops to the floor inside the stall, his head and bare shoulders appearing in the gap beneath the door.
"Wait. What do you mean?" Chandler asks from the floor.
Susie steps back into the doorway holding the clothes. "What do I mean? What do you mean, what do I mean? I mean underpants, mister. That's what I mean," she says loudly.
Chandler remains on the floor, looking toward the door.
"What do you mean?" Chandler asks again.
Susie stands in the doorway, keeping her gaze fixed on him.
"My skirt, you lifted," Susie says, her volume rising.
"Kids laughing," Susie says loudly, shaking her head.
"I was Susie Underpants till I was 18," Susie shouts, stepping slightly forward.
Chandler stands up, his bare chest and head appearing over the top of the stall door.
"That was in the fourth grade," Chandler says, gripping the top of the door.
"How could you still be upset about that?" Chandler asks, looking over the stall door.
"Well, why don't you call me in 20 years and tell me if you're still upset about it?" Susie yells, pointing at him.
Susie exits the bathroom, letting the heavy door swing shut behind her. Chandler watches her leave, his hands gripping the top of the stall.
"All right. I hope you realize you're not getting his underpants back," Chandler shouts toward the closed door.
Chandler remains leaning over the door, staring toward the exit as audience laughter builds.
Chandler turns his head, looking around the empty bathroom. His jaw tightens and his lips press together as the laughter continues.
At night on a city street, Monica and a man in a camouflage jacket walk side-by-side toward the foreground. Other people are visible walking in the background.
Monica smiles and looks at the man. "Can't believe this. Just like two weeks ago, I was watching Sudden Death," she says, laughing slightly while speaking.
"and now I'm on a date with Jean-Claude Van Damme," Monica continues, maintaining her smile as they walk.
Monica points her right index finger toward a person walking past them.
"Can you beat up that guy?" Monica asks, keeping her finger pointed.
The man nods and replies, "Yeah," though it is not subtitled. Monica lowers her hand, then points toward another off-screen figure.
"Can you beat up that guy?" Monica asks, looking toward the off-screen figure.
The man replies, "Sure." Monica smiles broadly and says, "This is so," cutting off before finishing her sentence.
Monica continues smiling and laughing lightly, her shoulders raised briefly as they stop walking.
The man stops walking, turns to face Monica, and says, "You know. I gotta admit, I was kind of surprised that you agreed to go on a blind date."
"Normally, I would not do it," the man adds. Monica raises her eyebrows and asks, "Yeah?"
"Well, what made you make the exception for me?" Monica asks. The man maintains eye contact.
The man replies, "Because Rachel told me you were dying to have a threesome with me and Drew," continuing to look at her.
"Barrymore," the man continues.
Monica's smile vanishes, her eyes widening as she stares at him.
The man points his index finger upward and adds, "Oh, by the way, Drew has some ground rules."
Monica glares off-screen with a tense jaw and narrowed eyes. The setting changes to an apartment living area where Phoebe, Monica, and Rachel stand near a kitchen.
Monica chases Rachel around the living area. Phoebe stands nearby with her arms raised. "Say you're sorry," Monica shouts.
Rachel continues to retreat toward the kitchen area as Monica pursues her.
"No," Rachel shouts back.
Monica grabs Rachel's arms. Rachel struggles to pull away as Phoebe steps toward them, holding out her hands.
"Say it, Rachel," Monica demands loudly, holding Rachel. "No, I'm not," Rachel replies, leaning backward over a kitchen chair.
Monica pulls a white sweater over Rachel's head. Rachel struggles, pulling her arms away.
Rachel breaks free and steps back, adjusting her hair. Monica holds the sweater up in front of her.
Holding the white sweater with both hands, Monica says, "Rachel. You say you're sorry or your sweater gets it."
Rachel stands a few feet away, holding her hands out. "Okay," she replies.
Rachel points both index fingers at Monica and says, "That is my favorite sweater. That is my third date sweater," her volume rising.
Monica grabs the neckline of the sweater with both hands, staring directly at Rachel.
"Say you're sorry," Monica repeats loudly.
Rachel lowers her hands, looking at Monica.
"Okay. You want to play?" Rachel asks, stepping toward the kitchen counter.
"Okay, let's play. Let's play," Rachel says, turning her back to grab a jar of marinara sauce from an overhead shelf.
Rachel turns around holding the jar and walks over to Monica's black handbag resting on the counter.
Rachel unscrews the lid of the jar. Monica pulls at a thread on the sweater, holding it taut.
"What are you going to do?" Monica asks. Holding the open jar over the handbag, Rachel replies, "You give me back my sweater or it's handbag marinara."
Phoebe stands watching with her hand over her mouth. Monica pulls the thread on the sweater further.
"You don't have the guts," Monica says, her lips pressed tightly together before speaking.
"Oh, yeah?" Rachel responds loudly.
Rachel tilts the jar slightly. "Well, at least I wasn't too chicken to tell some guy I thought he was. Shoot," she shouts.
Monica pulls the thread forcefully, causing the sweater sleeve to unravel significantly. Audience laughter sounds.
Rachel shouts, "Oh," with her eyes wide.
Rachel pours the red sauce into the handbag. Monica shrieks and pulls the thread continuously, unraveling a long piece of yarn while stomping her feet.
Rachel shakes the remaining sauce into the bag. Audience laughter continues as Monica groans, stretching the yarn out with both arms.
Phoebe steps forward, waving her hands. "Eric, stop the madness," she shouts loudly.
Rachel and Monica stop moving, both exhibiting heavy breathing. Rachel looks down at the jar. Monica stands holding the unraveled yarn.
"This is crazy. Who can even remember why you started fighting in the first place?" Phoebe asks, gesturing with her hands.
Rachel and Monica speak loudly at the same time, their lines overlapping in the subtitle: "She knew. But I, like, joined my baby. She wouldn't."
The overlapping shouting concludes with, "have you."
Phoebe nods her head rapidly with her eyes closed.
"Yes, that's right," Phoebe says, opening her eyes.
Rachel sets the empty jar down on the counter and looks toward the handbag.
Phoebe gestures with her hands open. "But still look at your purse," she says to Monica.
Monica looks down at the handbag.
"Look at your sweater," Phoebe says, turning toward Rachel.
Rachel looks at the unraveled sweater in Monica's hands.
Phoebe looks between them and says, "Look at yourself."
Silence falls. Rachel stares straight ahead with her mouth slightly open. Monica looks down, her shoulders slumped.
Monica looks down at the sweater in her hands. "I'll help you fix your sweater," she says softly.
Rachel wipes her forehead with her right hand.
Rachel looks at Monica and says, "I'll help you throw out your purse," speaking softer than her previous shouts.
Rachel picks up the stained handbag, while Monica holds out the damaged sweater. They hand the items to each other.
Monica holds the handbag and says, "I'm sorry that I made you stop seeing him."
Rachel looks down at the sweater in her hands.
"Well, I'm sorry I went out with him when I knew you liked him," Rachel replies, her gaze staying on the sweater.
Monica looks down at the handbag in her hands, her mouth closed and her gaze fixed downward.
Monica pulls a sauce-covered glove out of the handbag. "I'm sorry that I barge your glove," she says, holding it up.
Rachel and Phoebe stare silently at the sauce-covered glove Monica holds up as audience laughter sounds.
The setting changes to a public men's restroom with a row of pale blue stalls. Joey enters through a green door, walks forward, and begins to whistle a melody.
Joey stops whistling, pauses in front of a closed stall, and resumes whistling while rocking slightly on his heels.
Joey leans closer to the stall door, continuing to whistle. He stops abruptly and stares at the door.
Joey takes a step back from the stall, still looking at the door. He tilts his head slightly as audience laughter dies down.
An off-screen voice from inside the stall asks, "Joey."
Joey replies, "Mother," looking at the stall.
Joey turns his head quickly toward the stall door, his eyes widening in surprise as laughter sounds.
The off-screen voice from the stall repeats, "Joey."
Joey steps closer to the stall door and asks, "Chandler?" though it is not subtitled.
Joey says, "Chandler. What are you still doing here? I thought you guys took," pointing his index finger at the door.
The off-screen voice interrupts, saying, "no, no, no. She took off with my clothes." Joey lowers his hand.
Joey stares at the stall door with his mouth slightly open. Audience laughter builds.
Joey leans forward and asks, "Are you naked in there?" The off-screen voice replies, "Well, not exactly."
Joey stands still, looking toward the stall door as the clip ends.
In a public restroom, Joey leans against a gray stall door.
Joey looks at the stall door and asks, "Wearing panties?"
Joey gestures slightly with his hands, waiting for a response from inside the stall.
Joey asks, "You always wear panties?"
Joey stands still, listening with a neutral expression.
Chandler, speaking from off-screen inside the stall, says, "No, this is the first time."
Joey raises his eyebrows as audience laughter is heard.
Joey steps back slightly and says, "Wow. Talk about your bad mean."
Joey gestures with his hand and adds, "The first time you try panties and someone walks off with your clothes." Chandler's head appears over the top of the stall door amid audience laughter.
Chandler rests his arms on the top edge of the stall, looking down at Joey.
Chandler says, "I was not trying them out."
Chandler continues, "Susie asked me to wear them." Joey points his finger toward him.
Joey speaks and gestures toward the stall. Chandler shakes his head and lowers out of sight.
Joey steps forward, gripping the top edge of the stall door, and pulls himself up to look inside.
Joey smiles and nods as he looks over the door. The restroom door opens and Ross enters.
Ross walks into the restroom, stops, and stares at Joey.
Ross speaks to Joey. Joey turns to Ross, smiles, and points toward the stall.
Ross walks over and stands next to Joey. Both men grip the top of the stall door and look over it.
As Ross and Joey look over the stall door, voices exchange, "Let me see. No, no, you don't have to see."
Chandler stands up in the stall, his bare shoulders visible, prompting audience laughter. Ross and Joey smile at him.
A voice says, "Hi, tushy."
Chandler looks at Ross and Joey, his lips pressed together.
Chandler gestures and says, "All right, one of you give me your underpants." Joey gestures to himself, replying, "Oh, no. Can't help you. I'm not wearing any."
Ross and Chandler both turn to look at Joey in surprise. Audience laughter plays.
Ross asks, "How can you not be wearing any underwear?" Joey points toward Chandler and replies, "Oh, I'm getting heat from the guy in the hot pink thong." Audience laughter is heard.
Chandler closes his eyes briefly.
Chandler turns to Ross and says, "All right, look, Ross, I'll give you $50 for your underpants."
The three men continue talking over the stall door as an older man in a suit enters the bathroom.
The men notice the older man and fall silent. The setting changes to the restaurant dining area.
Phoebe sits alone at a dining table. Ross and Joey walk past her in the background.
Chandler follows them, holding a detached restroom stall door in front of his bare body to cover himself. He stops near a table as audience laughter plays.
Chandler speaks to the older man, who is now standing near the restaurant podium, then edges sideways past the seated diners.
Outside the Central Perk coffee shop, a sign with the logo is visible on the window. Inside, Phoebe, Ross, and Chandler sit on couches.
Chandler asks, "Phoebes, can I have the milk after you?" Phoebe pours milk into her cup and replies, "I'm almost done with it. Keep your panties on."
Chandler gives a tight-lipped smile. The coffee shop door opens, and Joey enters wearing a coat and scarf.
Joey walks toward the seating area, taking off his scarf, and announces, "Hey. And I'm in the movie."
Ross asks, "Hey, what happened?" Joey smiles widely and says, "One of the virus victims called in sick so Kathy recommended me, and boom. I'm dying on a gurney." Audience laughter is audible.
Chandler looks at Joey and says, "Hey."
Joey turns his attention to Ross.
Joey points to Ross and says, "Oh, Ross. Marcel just finished his last scene, if you want to go down there and say goodbye." Ross replies, "That's okay. I mean, he's probably got"
Ross continues, "parties to go to and stuff."
Ross shrugs slightly, his gaze lowered.
Ross says, "He's moved on. Hey, that's the way it goes, right?"
Outside the window behind the couch, a monkey (Marcel) presses its hands against the glass, looking inside.
Phoebe looks at the window and says, "Oh, my God."
Ross turns his head toward Phoebe.
Ross looks at Phoebe and asks, "What?"
The monkey continues pressing its hands against the glass, moving its arms slightly.
Ross turns to look at the window. The setting changes to a city street.
Ross walks down the sidewalk carrying the monkey on his shoulder, passing pedestrians.
Ross and the monkey stop at a newsstand where the monkey holds a newspaper reading "Daily Variety." Later, they sit on a bench, and the monkey touches a seated man's hand.
A vocal song begins with the lyrics, "Looks like we made it." Ross and the monkey stand near a movie poster on a brick wall.
The monkey climbs onto the ledge next to the poster as Ross smiles.
The song continues, "Where? I thought so till today until you were there everywhere," as Ross walks down the street eating an ice cream cone with the monkey on his shoulder.
The song lyrics play, "and all I could taste is love the way we made it," as Ross looks at the monkey.
The shot focuses closely on Ross's face as the clip ends.
Ross walks along a city street holding a pretzel, with the monkey Marcel perched on his shoulder. A vocal song plays in the background.
Ross smiles and looks toward Marcel. The song lyrics "look like we made it" play.
The setting changes to a view of a city skyline over a river at sunset. A tugboat moves across the water.
The setting changes to a street outside a building. Jean-Claude Van Damme stands facing Monica and Rachel.
Jean-Claude gestures with his hands and says, "I'm sorry it didn't work out between you and me. Or you and me. Drew was very disappointed." Monica and Rachel stand side-by-side, listening.
Monica and Rachel smile and look at each other.
Rachel says, "Okay, well, bye," and steps toward Jean-Claude.
Rachel wraps her arms around Jean-Claude's neck and they kiss. Monica stands nearby, watching. Rachel then pulls back.
Jean-Claude says, "Goodbye," to Rachel as she turns to leave.
Monica steps forward, facing Jean-Claude.
Monica says, "Well, bye for me too," while looking up at Jean-Claude.
Monica wraps her arms around Jean-Claude's neck, and they kiss.
Rachel stops walking and turns around, watching Monica and Jean-Claude kiss. The two separate.
Rachel walks back toward Jean-Claude, saying, "Okay, well, bye bye again," and gesturing with her hand.
Rachel steps up to Jean-Claude, puts her hands on his shoulders, and kisses him again.
Rachel pulls back. Monica steps closer and says, "Okay," looking at them.
Monica leans in and quickly kisses Jean-Claude again.
Jean-Claude brings his hands together and says, "Perhaps the three of us just could—" while Monica and Rachel shake their heads and simultaneously say, "Oh, no."
Monica and Rachel continue shaking their heads, raising their hands.
Jean-Claude brings his fists together and asks, "Are you sure I can crush a walnut with my butt?" Monica and Rachel immediately reply, "Oh, no," continuing to shake their heads.
Rachel says, "Impressive, but no." Monica smiles and adds, "Maybe if I were baking. Yes."
Jean-Claude nods slightly and says, "Okay."
Monica and Rachel turn around to leave.
Monica and Rachel say, "Bye. Bye," as they walk away.
Jean-Claude watches Monica and Rachel walk down the street. The setting changes to another outdoor street location. A blonde woman holds Marcel near an open dark car, while Ross stands nearby.
Ross looks at the monkey and says, "Bye, Marcel."
Ross raises a hand, pointing a finger toward Marcel.
Ross says, "See, on the big screen," keeping his finger pointed.
Ross continues looking at Marcel with his hand slightly raised.
Ross lowers his hand and says, "You keep people drinking that beer. Okay." Chandler, Phoebe, Monica, Rachel, and Joey stand behind him.
The woman lowers Marcel toward the open door of the car.
Ross extends his hand. Marcel grasps his finger as Ross says, "Miss you, buddy."
The woman places Marcel inside the car and closes the door. Ross and the rest of the group watch quietly.
Ross steps closer to the car, looking down through the open sunroof. The others stand behind him, looking in the same direction.
Inside the car, Marcel presses his hands against the rear window, looking out. Ross raises his hand in a wave.
Ross keeps his hand raised as the car drives away down the street. The group continues to watch.
The car turns a corner. The setting changes to Ross, Chandler, Phoebe, Monica, Rachel, and Joey walking together down a city sidewalk.
Phoebe says, "I think I want to write a song about all this." Chandler turns his head toward her and asks, "Oh, yeah?"
The group continues walking along the sidewalk.
Phoebe looks forward and says, "Oh, except one of the strings on my guitar is broken."
Phoebe turns toward Chandler and asks, "Hey, Chandler, can I borrow your g-string?" Audience laughter is audible.
Chandler looks at Phoebe as audience laughter continues.
Chandler asks, "How long you been waiting to say that?" Phoebe smiles and replies, "About 20 minutes."
The group walks past a street sign.
The setting changes to a movie set where Joey lies flat on a stretcher. Jean-Claude wears a military uniform, and several people in hazmat suits stand around them.
Jean-Claude leans over Joey and says, "Can't you see what's going on here? This man is dying."
Joey tilts his head back and lets out a long, loud groan.
An off-screen voice shouts, "Cut." Joey instantly stops groaning and lowers his head.
The scene resets slightly, showing the start of another take. Joey lies flat on the stretcher.
Jean-Claude leans in again and says, "Can't you see what's going on here? This man is dying."
Joey lets out a high-pitched, strained wail, saying, "Ah."
An off-screen voice shouts, "Cut." Joey stops wailing. The scene resets to another take with Joey lying completely still.
Jean-Claude leans over Joey and says, "Can't you see what's going on here?"
Jean-Claude finishes, "This man is dying."
Joey lets out a loud, trembling wail, clenching his eyes shut and shifting his head.
Joey groans the word, "Mommy," while keeping his eyes squeezed shut. Audience laughter is audible.
The scene resets. Joey lies still on the stretcher with his eyes closed.
Jean-Claude stands over Joey and says, "Can you see what's going on? This man is dead."
Jean-Claude pulls a white sheet up over Joey's head. Joey remains motionless beneath the sheet.
The people in hazmat suits stand still around the stretcher. The visual turns completely black.
The screen remains black.
